LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS CHRISTOPHER SCHULTZ AND KIMBERLEY KIRCHMEYER'S DEMURRER TO THE FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT'S FIFTH AND SIXTH CAUSES OF ACTION IS STAYED PENDING DETERMINATION OF PLAINTIFF KENNETH STOLLER'S MOTION TO FILE A SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT. ORDER IS SIGNED. (SEE SCANNED ORDER FOR DETAILS) JUDGE: RICHARD B. ULMER JR., CLERK: M. GOODMAN, COURT REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR#8600, MARIA.TORREANO@GMAIL.COM, REPORTED.
SUMMONS ISSUED ON 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT TO PLAINTIFF STOLLER MD, KENNETH P K, J BEING THE MOTHER OF A VACCINE EXEMPT CHILD

MEMORANDUM OF POINTS AND AUTHORITIES IN OPPOSITION TO DEFENDANTS DEMURRER TO THE SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINTORT OF MOTION FOR LEAVE TO FILE SAC (TRANSACTION ID # 100089310) FILED BY PLAINTIFF STOLLER MD, KENNETH P

LAW & MOTION, DEPT. 302, AS TO DEC-03-2019 HEARING RE: DEFENDANT KIMBERLY KIRCHMEYER, CHRISTOPHER SCHULTZ'S DEMURRER AND REQUEST FOR JUDICAIL NOTICE TO UNVERIFIED SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT FOR DECLARATORY AND INJUNCTIVE RELIEF, THE COURT ADOPTS THE TENTATIVE RULING. DEFENDANTS KIMBERLY KIRCHMEYER AND CHRISTOPHER SHULTZ'S DEMURRER TO THE SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT'S FIRST, SECOND AND THIRD CAUSES OF ACTION IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND. PLAINTIFFS EFFECTIVELY CONCEDE THAT THEY HAVE FAILED TO EXHAUST ADMINISTRATIVE REMEDIES. (SEE CONTRACTORS' STATE LICENSE BOARD V. SUP. CT. (2018) 28 CAL.APP.5TH 771, 778-79.) PLAINTIFFS NONETHELESS URGE ME TO BYPASS THE ADMINISTRATIVE PROCEEDINGS USING A "PUBLIC POLICY EXCEPTION" THAT APPELLATE COURTS SOMETIMES INVOKE, USUALLY WITHOUT CALLING IT THAT. HOWEVER, I BELIEVE A FACTUAL RECORD DEVELOPED IN THE ADMINISTRATIVE PROCEEDINGS WOULD BE BENEFICIAL TO REASONED ADJUDICATION OF THIS DISPUTE. ADDING "JK" AS A PLAINTIFF DOES NOT AVOID THE EXHAUSTION BAR; SHE MAY SEEK TO INTERVENE IN THE ADMINISTRATIVE PROCEEDINGS. (GOVT. CODE 11440.50. JUDGE: RICHARD B. ULMER JR., CLERK: M. GOODMAN, COURT REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR#8600, MARIA.TORREANO@GMAIL.COM, REPORTED. (302/RBU)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Dec-23-2019 Continued To Feb-19-20 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Dec-03-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT KIMBERLY KIRCHMEYER, CHRISTOPHER SCHULTZ'S DEMURRER AND REQUEST FOR JUDICAIL NOTICE TO UNVERIFIED SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT FOR DECLARATORY AND INJUNCTIVE RELIEF.

Defendants Kimberly Kirchmeyer and Christopher Shultz's demurrer to the second amended complaint's first, second and third causes of action is sustained without leave to amend. Plaintiffs effectively concede that they have failed to exhaust administrative remedies. (See Contractors' State License Board v. Sup. Ct. (2018) 28 Cal.App.5th 771, 778-79.) Plaintiffs nonetheless urge me to bypass the administrative proceedings using a "public policy exception" that appellate courts sometimes invoke, usually without calling it that. However, I believe a factual record developed in the administrative proceedings would be beneficial to reasoned adjudication of this dispute. Adding "JK" as a plaintiff does not avoid the exhaustion bar; she may seek to intervene in the administrative proceedings. (Govt. Code 11440.50.)

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr., Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/RBU)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Oct-22-2019 Continued To Jan-08-20 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Motion For Leave To File A Second Amended Complaint
Off Calendar Oct-07-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Richard A. Jaffe, Esq., T:713.857.8096, for plaintiff and moving party, by Court Call;
No other appearances.

RULING: There being no objection, the Court adopted the tentative ruling on PLAINTIFF KENNETH STOLLER's Motion For Leave To File A Second Amended Complaint, as follows:

Plaintiff Kenneth P. Stoller, M.D.'s motion for leave to file a second amended complaint is granted. (See, e.g., Howard v. County of San Diego (2010) 184 Cal.App.4th 1422, 1428 ["The policy favoring amendment is so strong that it is a rare case in which denial of leave to amend can be justified."].) The parties shall meet and confer before Defendants file a demurrer to the second amended complaint. (Code Civ. Proc. 430.41.) The validity of that proposed complaint, which differs very substantially from the existing first amended complaint, can then be addressed on the basis of a clear record. (See Brown v. Ralphs Grocery Co. (2018) 28 Cal.App.5th 824, 843 ["The 'better course of action' is to allow a plaintiff to amend the complaint 'and then let the parties test its legal sufficiency in other appropriate proceedings.'"].)

Order signed in open court.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Court Reporter: Maria A. Torreano CSR#8600, email: maria.torreano@gmail.com

RULING - The matter is Discussed. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT CHRISTOPHER SCHULTZ, KIMBERLY KIRCHMEYER'S REQUEST FOR JUDICIAL NOTICE, DEMURRER TO VERIFIED 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT FOR DECLARATORY AND INJUNCTIVE RELIEF.
